source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager - German Speaking, Fine Jewellery (Europe)
source_jobs.description:
Role: Business Development Manager - German Speaking (Europe)

Location: Remote in Europe

Salary: Competitive base + Commission

Team: Buy-side Sales





ABOUT NIVODA 🚀

Nivoda is a rapidly scaling global B2B diamond and gemstone marketplace connecting jewellery retailers with competitive, high-quality suppliers worldwide. Processing $300M+ in annual transactions and operating across multiple regions, Nivoda is transforming a $350B industry that has historically been slow to modernise.

Our platform gives businesses of all sizes, from independent jewellers to major retail chains access to the world's best diamond and gemstone inventory, without the traditional barriers of capital, geography, or information asymmetry.




OUR MISSION 🎯

Diamond and gemstone trading has long been plagued by inefficiency, opacity, and exclusivity. Nivoda was built to change that - to democratise access and empower every business, regardless of size or location, to buy and sell with confidence. Independent jewellers are at the heart of this mission. They deserve the same access to quality, pricing, and selection as the biggest players in the industry. That's exactly what we give them.




THE TEAM ⭐️

Our Sales team is the engine that brings Nivoda's mission to life. We're a high-energy, globally distributed group that moves fast, owns outcomes, and genuinely cares about the success of every jewellery business we work with. This is a team that thrives on commercial creativity, deep customer relationships, and relentless execution and we're growing fast.




ABOUT THE ROLE 🤔

As a Business Development Manager (BDM), you will be at the forefront of this growth, leading the charge in transforming potential into partnership and partnership into exponential growth. This role is not just about hitting targets; it’s about redefining what’s possible in the world’s largest diamond marketplace.

Our core DNA: we start with the customer and seek to understand. You will be the architect of customer growth and success, guiding clients from their first interaction with Nivoda to becoming lifelong advocates of our platform. Your influence will span the entire customer lifecycle, ensuring that every touchpoint is an opportunity to deliver value and drive growth.

Native or professional proficiency in English and German is required for this role.




WHAT WILL YOU BE DOING? 😎

 - Inspire and Onboard New Customers: Engage with high-potential prospects in the DACH region, understanding their unique needs, and crafting compelling narratives that position Nivoda as their go-to marketplace. Partner with SDRs to seamlessly transition leads into active buyers.

 - Champion Customer Success: Serve as the trusted advisor to your clients, building deep, value-driven relationships. You will be their strategic partner, anticipating their needs and proactively driving solutions that deliver unmatched ROI.

 - Drive Strategic Growth: Identify and execute on opportunities to expand and deepen client engagement through up-selling and cross-selling initiatives. You will turn customer success into business growth, aligning Nivoda’s offerings with the evolving needs of our clients.

 - Collaborative Excellence: Work hand-in-hand with SDRs, TAMs, and CS teams to ensure every customer journey is seamless and every handoff is flawless. You will be the linchpin that aligns efforts across teams, driving efficiency and maximising customer satisfaction.

 - Data-Driven Performance: Leverage key metrics such as GMV, Active Buyers, First Purchases, Retention, Frequency, ARPU, and LTV to refine strategies, optimise performance, and contribute to the continuous improvement of our platform.

 - Innovate for Impact: Actively collaborate with product teams, providing insightful customer feedback that drives innovation and positions Nivoda as the leader in the marketplace. You’ll be a catalyst for change, always pushing for better.
   
    


WHAT DO WE NEED FROM YOU? 💪

 - Fluent in German & En

source_jobs.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
🏢 ABOUT NIVODA

Nivoda is a fast-growing B2B marketplace transforming the global diamond and gemstone industry. We connect thousands of suppliers and retailers across 100+ countries, processing $300M+ in annual transactions - and we're just getting started. The gemstone industry is a $350 billion opportunity, and we're building the infrastructure to unlock it.

We're at an inflection point - scaling fast, hiring deliberately, and rethinking how we build. We're not a company that moves slowly and ships carefully. We ship, learn, and iterate - and we're looking for engineers who love that rhythm.




🎯 OUR MISSION

The traditional diamond supply chain is opaque, slow, and inaccessible to anyone without deep industry connections. We're changing that. Nivoda's mission is to make the global gemstone market accessible to every business, regardless of size or location. We democratise access to inventory, pricing, and logistics so that a jeweller in Boston has the same power as a trading house in Antwerp.

Engineering is how we make that happen. The code you write will be used by tens of thousands of buyers and suppliers every day.




⭐️ THE GROWTH TEAM

You'll be joining the Growth pod - a high-priority, high-visibility squad that owns the levers that compound: acquisition, conversion, retention, and monetisation across the entire customer lifecycle. This is a team where engineering decisions show up in the growth dashboard the next morning.

The pod operates like a startup inside Nivoda. You'll work closely with the PM and designer, move fast, run experiments, and have a real say in how things get built. We're hybrid in Boston - you'll be in with the team regularly, and we think the best ideas happen in the room.

This pod is also where Nivoda is deliberately changing how we build. We're investing in AI-assisted engineering workflows, giving engineers real latitude to try new tools, and building a culture where experimentation is the default - not the exception. If you want to be part of shaping that, this is the team to join.




🤝 ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Full-Stack Engineer on the Growth pod, you'll own end-to-end implementation of features that directly move revenue, conversion, and retention metrics. You'll work across the stack - TypeScript and Node.js on the backend, React on the frontend - and be expected to go wherever the problem takes you.

This isn't a role where you pick up tickets and ship them. You'll be involved in shaping what gets built and how, contributing to technical decisions, and helping define what good engineering looks like in a team that's moving fast. You'll also be one of the people leading the way on how Nivoda uses AI in its engineering workflow - not just as a productivity tool, but as a genuine lever for building better software faster.




🚀 WHAT WOULD YOU BE DOING?

 - Build and ship features across the full stack that directly grow revenue, conversion, and retention - you'll see your work in the metrics within days of shipping.

 - Design and implement the technical architecture for growth features, in-app experiments, and lifecycle and monetisation flows - making pragmatic decisions that balance speed with maintainability.

 - Identify and build growth loops - referral mechanics, pricing and packaging experiments, and features that compound usage across both sides of the marketplace.

 - Use AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude, Copilot, or whatever works best for you) as a first-class part of your engineering workflow - we actively encourage this and want to learn from how you do it.

 - Contribute to how the team uses AI beyond your own workflow - sharing patterns, tools, and approaches that make the whole squad faster and better.

 - Work closely with the PM and designer in tight, fast feedback loops - you're not waiting for specs to be 'done' before you start thinking about implementation.

 - Run A/B experiments end-to-end: implement the test, instrument the events, analyse the results, and

source_jobs.title: Senior Finance Integrations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
💎 NIVODA: WHERE INNOVATION AND GEMSTONES MEET



🚀 ABOUT NIVODA

Nivoda is a fast-growing B2B marketplace that is transforming the global diamond and gemstone industry. We connect buyers and sellers across the world, processing $300M+ in annual transactions and unlocking a $350B industry opportunity. We move fast, think big, and care deeply about the businesses and people we serve.




🌍 OUR MISSION

The diamond trading industry has historically been fragmented, opaque, and inaccessible. Nivoda was founded to change that: to empower businesses of all sizes and locations to participate in a fair, efficient global marketplace. Every line of code, every integration, every automation we build brings us closer to that mission.




⭐️ THE ENGINEERING TEAM

Our Engineering team is at the core of everything Nivoda does. We build the platform, the integrations, and the infrastructure that power the marketplace. We operate with a low-hierarchy, high-ownership culture: engineers own their work end to end, from design to deployment to monitoring. We're remote-first, collaborative, and obsessed with building things that actually matter to the business.




🤔 ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Finance Integration Engineer to own the critical connection between Nivoda's platform and our financial systems, primarily NetSuite. This is a pivotal role: our NetSuite integration is currently broken, and we need someone who can fix it, rebuild it properly, and ensure data flows correctly and reliably across our stack. You'll sit at the intersection of engineering and finance, working closely with finance stakeholders to understand the domain and translate business requirements into robust, automated integrations. This is not a NetSuite consulting role: we need a strong backend engineer who happens to know NetSuite, not the other way around.




😎 WHAT WOULD YOU BE DOING?

 - Own the end-to-end integration between Nivoda's platform and NetSuite: design, build, deploy, monitor, and maintain.

 - Diagnose and fix the existing NetSuite integration issues, ensuring data accuracy and reliability across the financial pipeline.

 - Build automated data flows that push and pull financial data between Nivoda's platform and NetSuite, eliminating manual processes.

 - Collaborate closely with the Finance team and key stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into robust engineering solutions.

 - Design and implement monitoring, alerting, and testing to proactively catch integration failures before they impact the business.

 - Work within Nivoda's existing tech stack (Node.js, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines) to deliver scalable, maintainable solutions.

 - Proactively reach out to stakeholders when you need clarification, you don't wait for answers, you go find them.

 - Take ownership of the integration roadmap, working with the Product and Engineering Managers to balance data correctness and accuracy with coverage for new product flows and attributes.

 - Contribute to engineering best practices: code reviews, documentation, and raising the bar for the team.

 - Be the go-to person for anything at the intersection of financial systems and Nivoda's platform.




😉 WHAT DO WE NEED FROM YOU?

 - Strong backend engineering background: you are a software engineer first, with experience in object-oriented programming (Node.js preferred)

 - Hands-on experience integrating with NetSuite, you've worked with NetSuite APIs, understand how data flows in and out, and can navigate the platform confidently.

 - Solid understanding of accounting and finance fundamentals: you don't need to be a CFO, but you need to understand how financial data works and why accuracy matters.

 - Experience building and maintaining integrations using AWS services, Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.

 - A track record of owning integrations end to end: you monitor, test, and fix your own work without being asked.

 - Strong stakeholder communication skills:

source_jobs.title: Fulfilment Executive
source_jobs.description:
FULFILMENT EXECUTIVE – HONG KONG



We are looking for an experienced QC & Logistics Associate to join our Hong Kong office. In this role, you will be responsible for managing all incoming and outgoing diamond and jewellery shipments, ensuring timely movement and maintaining the highest standards of quality control.

The primary objective is to ensure diamonds and jewellery flow in and out of the Hong Kong office with minimal delays while ensuring every item meets Nivoda’s quality standards.

 


ABOUT NIVODA

Nivoda is a young and energetic global team headquartered in London with offices in Mumbai, New York, Hong Kong, Amsterdam, Melbourne, Antwerp, and Dubai.

We are a fast-growing B2B marketplace transforming how the global jewelry industry operates. Through our online platform, we connect buyers and sellers of jewelry and provide the most transparent, efficient, and cost-effective way to source and sell diamonds and jewelry.

Nivoda’s growth has been exponential—over 250% in sales growth in the last 12 months, with our team expanding from 30 to 450+ employees globally. We are a team of big thinkers, problem solvers, and go-getters building the future of the jewelry supply chain.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Hand-deliver diamonds to local customers when needed

 - Collect diamonds and jewellery from local suppliers, perform quality checks, and prepare items for shipment

 - Perform jewellery QC, including checking for manufacturing defects, confirming product specifications (weight, size, finish), and ensuring stones are securely set

 - Identify any discrepancies between products received and purchase order specifications (stone quality, metal type, craftsmanship, etc.)

 - Inspect jewellery for damage, scratches, loose stones, and overall finishing before dispatch

 - Create shipments using the Nivoda platform and send labels to suppliers to ensure timely dispatch

 - Receive consolidated shipments via Brinks, UPS, FedEx, etc.

 - Mark orders as received in the Nivoda system

 - Liaise with suppliers regarding shipments and forward invoices to the finance team

 - Assist customer support teams with shipping queries, order updates, and urgent delivery requests

 - Support smooth daily operations of the Hong Kong office

 - Handle general administrative tasks as needed

 - Follow up with carriers (Brinks, FedEx, UPS, etc.) regarding shipment delays

 - Process returned diamonds and jewellery from customers

 - Contribute to the continuous improvement of local office processes and workflows
   


WHAT YOU’LL NEED

 - Minimum 2 years of diamond and/or jewellery quality control experience

 - Experience managing stock or inventory of high-value products

 - Strong understanding of jewellery craftsmanship standards, stone setting types, metal types, and common QC defects

 - Logistics experience or familiarity with Brinks, FedEx, UPS, and tracking systems (preferred)

 - Strong attention to detail

 - High levels of honesty, professionalism, and integrity
   


WHAT YOU’LL GET

 - Opportunity to join a fast-growing global company

 - Exposure to international operations and best practices

 - Unlimited holiday

 - A dynamic, collaborative, and supportive work environment

source_jobs.title: Operations Associate - Media
source_jobs.description:
Designation: Operations Associate (Media)

Experience: 0-2 years

Location: Bangkok (Work from Office)



About Nivoda:

Nivoda’s growth trajectory has skyrocketed over the past 12 months, and we continue to expand across new regions at an unprecedented pace. Our DNA centers on understanding our customers' needs and offering them a seamless experience. With a growing team based in major cities worldwide, including London, New York, Hong Kong, and Mumbai, Nivoda is becoming the go-to marketplace for the global jewelry industry.

We are bold, innovative, and relentless in our pursuit of excellence as we transform an industry steeped in tradition.

To know more about us, please visit http://www.nivoda.netwww.nivoda.com http://www.nivoda.com



What you'll do:

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

 - Handle day to day operations of photography machines

 - Follow the guidelines and SOPs

 - (Media)Record keeping

 - Ensure efficient operations/maintenance of machines
   
   

What you'll need:

 - Self-motivated/can-do attitude

What you'll get:

 - Dynamic working environment in an extremely fast-growing company

 - Work in an international environment

 - Ample experience to grow and learn.

 - A great company culture.

 - Unlimited holiday allowance

source_jobs.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Nivoda: Where Innovation and Gemstones Meet
At Nivoda, we are passionate about empowering jewelry retailers and gemstone suppliers to thrive in today's dynamic market. As the leading B2B diamond and gemstone marketplace, we are dedicated to providing an exceptional platform that connects jewellery businesses of all sizes with the global diamond supply.
Our team of over 400 dedicated employees, many with a wealth of industry experience, have meticulously developed our award-winning platform that addresses the unique challenges of the jewellery sector. With Nivoda, you can buy and sell diamonds securely, efficiently, hassle-free, and at the most competitive prices.

Engineering At Nivoda
Technology is at the heart of Nivoda's business, powering everything we do. Within our remote-first team, we foster a culture of innovation and collaboration where engineers can thrive. Join us and be part of a dynamic environment that values creativity, empowers individuality, and recognizes excellence. Together, we push boundaries to deliver groundbreaking solutions and leave a lasting impact on the global industry.

About the role
At Nivoda, we're passionate about pushing the boundaries of innovation to empower our customers in the online jewelry industry. As a Senior Fullstack Engineer, you'll be at the forefront of this mission, collaborating directly with our business stakeholders and engineers to architect, develop, and launch groundbreaking products. Your expertise will span the full stack: from crafting intuitive, high-performance frontend experiences to designing robust backend systems that power them.

The impact you will have on the role

 - Design, implement, and maintain fullstack features for high-performance, customer-centric experiences across the Nivoda platform, ensuring scalability, reliability, and security.

 - Build and iterate on frontend interfaces that are fast, accessible, and delightful to use.

 - Ensure adherence to best practices, coding standards, and quality guidelines throughout the development lifecycle.

 - Collaborate with product managers, architects, and stakeholders to define technical requirements and translate business needs into technical solutions.

 - Debug and resolve complex technical issues, providing timely support to internal teams and external customers.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and prioritize customer requirements and feedback.

 - Mentor junior developers and improve the quality of engineering work in the broader team.

 - Actively participate in discussions to improve the overall customer experience on the Nivoda platform.

Minimum Qualifications and experience:

 - 5+ years of relevant experience in fullstack development, with strong proficiency in Node.js on the backend.

 - Solid frontend experience with a modern framework: we use React.

 - At least 2 years of experience working with GraphQL.

 - Strong foundation in database concepts, with at least 4 years of experience working with relational databases like PostgreSQL.

 - Proficient in Test-Driven Development (TDD) and committed to writing high-quality, maintainable code.

 - Good analytical and problem-solving capabilities.

 - B.S or M.S Tech degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering.

What we offer

 - Dynamic working environment in a rapidly growing company.

 - Work on cloud-hosted applications utilizing major AWS services.

 - Gain valuable experience in system design and architectural patterns.

 - Enjoy a pleasant, low-hierarchy work environment.

 - Engage in intellectually challenging work that contributes significantly to Nivoda's success and scalability.

 - Flexible working hours and a vibrant company culture.

 - Plenty of opportunities for growth and learning.

 - Unlimited holiday allowance.

 - Chance to join and contribute to a company during its exponential expansion phase.

source_jobs.title: FP&A Associate
source_jobs.description:
Location: Stockholm, London, or Remote EU

Function: Finance / FP&A

Employment type: Full-time

Reporting Line: Finance, working closely with the CFO, FP&A Lead and Accounting, as well as our Go-to-Market- and EPD leadership.




ABOUT THE COMPANY

We are a fast-growing AI-enabled B2B Marketplace and FinTech platform with a global footprint spanning the US, Europe, India and APAC. We connect online and offline businesses across the Jewellery industry, streamline complex diamond, gemstones and jewellery supply chains and logistics corridors, and process high transaction volumes across multiple currencies and payment rails. As we continue to scale, having sharp strategic financial analysis and decision support are critical enablers of our growth.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for an FP&A Associate who wants to move into a high visibility, operational role from the world of IB and / or VC finance, and use that move to compound their craft by working inside a fast-paced, high-growth, technology-led platform business. This role is ideal for someone who has spent 2 to 4 years in investment banking, private equity, venture capital or other similar corporate finance roles, and now wants to learn how a business actually gets built: how cohorts compound, where unit economics break, which GTM motions earn the right to scale, and what board-grade narratives and analyses look like when it is your job to own them.

You will own cohort- and growth analytics for the company, partner with our Go-to-Market team to find efficiencies and unlock compounding growth, and make sure that core reporting to management, the wider company, and our Board lands on time and at quality. You will help take our FP&A function from 1 to 5; we have broken ground, and you will help build the rest.

You will also be one of the early operators inside an AI-native finance and data function. The team works mainly with Anthropic’s Claude platform, with Snowflake as our data warehouse, NetSuite as our ERP, and ThoughtSpot as our BI layer. You can be based in Stockholm, London or Remote EU, and you will work closely with the CFO, FP&A Lead, Accounting, Treasury and our GTM leadership.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Cohort Modelling & Growth Analytics: Build and maintain cohort models across customers, suppliers, product lines (jewellery, diamonds, gemstones) and regions

Decompose growth into rate and base effects (new vs. existing, price vs. volume, mix shifts, FX) so we know which lever is doing the work.

Produce the analytics that determine where we lean in, where we cut, and where we wait.

2. GTM Partnership & Commercial Efficiency: Partner with Sales, Account Management, Marketing and RevOps to build the operating cadence: pipeline math, conversion rates, ramp curves, productivity per rep, CAC and LTV by segment.

Identify where the GTM machine is leaking efficiency (channel mix, segment ROI, discounting, comp plan distortions, hand-off points) and work with the team to fix it.

Build the business cases for hiring, territory expansion, TAM/SAM/SOM, pricing changes and new GTM motions, and stay close enough to the team to know if the assumptions are holding.

3. Core Reporting & Cadence: In partnership with / by leveraging the Finance teams AI agents, own the core management reporting pack: recurring KPI read-outs, monthly P&L and KPI commentary, and quarterly business reviews. Make sure they land on time, every time.

Build and maintain the company-wide reporting that goes to the team in our all-hands and async updates, so everyone has a shared view of how we are doing.

Co-own the board reporting cycle with the rest of FP&A: The deck, the data behind it, the commentary, and the running narrative quarter to quarter.

4. Forecasting, Planning & Budget Cycles: Maintain and improve our short-term forecasts (monthly re-forecasts) and the annual operating plan, in partnership with department leads.

Translate operational assumptions into a coherent P&L, balance sheet

source_jobs.title: Senior Fulfilment Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for an experienced logistics and operations professional to join our Mumbai team. You will play a key role in Nivoda's India fulfillment operations — managing inbound and outbound shipments, coordinating with suppliers, and ensuring our stock of diamonds and gemstones is handled with the highest standards of accuracy, security, and efficiency.




ABOUT US

Nivoda’s growth trajectory has skyrocketed over the past 12 months, and we continue to expand across new regions at an unprecedented pace. Our DNA centers on understanding our customers' needs and offering them a seamless experience. With a growing team based in major cities worldwide, including London, New York, Hong Kong, and Mumbai, Nivoda is becoming the go-to marketplace for the global jewelry industry.

We are bold, innovative, and relentless in our pursuit of excellence as we transform an industry steeped in tradition.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Manage inbound and outbound shipments to and from India, including documentation, carrier coordination, and exception handling.

 - Coordinate with suppliers across Mumbai and Surat for timely drop-offs and collections.

 - Own end-to-end fulfilment for Nivoda Express — managing the full order lifecycle from QC to final dispatch & returns.

 - Take complete ownership of Nivoda's physical stock of diamonds and gemstones, including inventory audits and reconciliations.

 - Handle returns, damaged goods, and shipment exceptions with precision and proper documentation.

 - Generate monthly and quarterly operational reports covering shipment volumes, collection performance, and carrier KPIs.

 - Own the creation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of SOPs across the fulfilment function — ensuring processes are clear, current, and consistently followed.

 - Contribute to Business Requirements Documents (BRDs), identify operational gaps, and lead process improvement initiatives that raise the bar for efficiency and accuracy across the fulfilment team.




BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–4 years of experience in logistics, supply chain, or operations.

 - Strong data analysis skills with the ability to generate clear, structured reports.

 - Excellent command of Excel — pivot tables, VLOOKUPs, etc.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills.

 - Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment while prioritising high-value shipments and tasks with precision.

 - Exceptional attention to detail, ensuring every item is handled with care and exacting standards.

 - High level of professionalism, discretion, and integrity when managing valuable assets.

 - Alignment with Nivoda's company values, culture, and commitment to excellence.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in the diamond, gemstone, or jewellery industry.

 - Familiarity with customs regulations, export/import documentation, or duty compliance.

 - Experience writing SOPs or BRDs in a cross-functional environment.

 - Exposure to BI or reporting tools (ThoughtSpot, Trevor, or similar).

 - Prior experience managing carrier relationships (FedEx, Brinks, or similar).

 - Familiarity with AI tools (e.g. Claude) to support reporting, documentation, and process improvement workflows.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
🏢 ABOUT NIVODA

Nivoda is a fast-growing B2B marketplace transforming the global diamond and gemstone industry. We connect thousands of suppliers and retailers across 100+ countries, processing $300M+ in annual transactions - and we're just getting started. The gemstone industry is a $350 billion opportunity, and we're building the infrastructure to unlock it.

We move fast, hire exceptional people, and give them real ownership over meaningful work. If you want to join a company where your design decisions show up in the numbers - and where craft actually matters - Nivoda is your place.




🎯 OUR MISSION

The traditional diamond supply chain is opaque, slow, and inaccessible to anyone without deep industry connections. We're changing that. Nivoda's mission is to make the global gemstone market accessible to every business, regardless of size or location - giving a jeweller in Boston the same power as a trading house in Antwerp.

Great design is central to that mission. We believe that how something works and how it feels are inseparable - and that the best product experiences create trust, reduce friction, and make complex things feel simple.




⭐️ THE GROWTH TEAM

You'll be embedded in the Growth pod - one of the highest-priority, highest-visibility squads at Nivoda. The pod owns the levers that compound: acquisition, conversion, retention, and monetisation across the entire customer lifecycle. It's a team where design decisions show up in the growth dashboard the next morning.

You'll work closely with Product Managers and engineers in the squad, and be part of a wider design team that cares about systems thinking, shipping fast, and making things people actually love to use.




🤝 ABOUT THE ROLE

As Senior Product Designer on the Growth pod, you'll own the design of some of the most important surfaces at Nivoda: the experiences that turn interest into revenue and keep customers coming back. From experiments that lift conversion to features that build lasting habits, your work will directly shape how the business grows.

This is a role for a designer who's equally comfortable with a blank canvas and a complex system - someone who can go from a rough problem brief to a polished prototype fast, who cares about both pixels and patterns, and who sees collaboration with PMs and engineers as a creative multiplier, not a constraint.




🚀 WHAT WOULD YOU BE DOING?

 - Own end-to-end design for growth surfaces across the customer lifecycle - working closely with the Growth PM to identify friction, run design experiments, and improve conversion, retention, and revenue.

 - Design growth loops and lifecycle experiences - referral mechanics, pricing and packaging surfaces, and features that compound usage across both sides of the marketplace.

 - Balance multiple workstreams and know when to go deep on craft versus move fast and ship.

 - Contribute to and maintain Nivoda's design system, ensuring consistency and quality across the pod and raising the bar for design coherence across the product.

 - Run lightweight user research - interviews, usability tests, and session reviews - to ground design decisions in real user behaviour rather than assumption.

 - Work directly with engineers to ensure designs ship accurately and at pace. You won't throw specs over the wall - you'll be in the build with them.

 - Use data and experimentation to validate design decisions: review funnel analytics, collaborate on A/B test design, and translate results into design iteration.

 - Communicate design rationale clearly to stakeholders - you can explain a decision in one sentence or a 10-minute presentation, depending on who's in the room.

 - Bring an AI-informed perspective to your workflow - whether that's using AI tools to accelerate ideation and prototyping, or thinking about where AI features can reduce friction for users.

 - Mentor more junior designers on the team and help build a culture of strong craft and feedback.




🔍 WHAT DO WE NEED FR

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Growth
source_jobs.description:
Senior Product Manager — Growth

 

🚀 About Nivoda
Nivoda is a fast-growing B2B marketplace transforming the global diamond and gemstone industry. We connect thousands of suppliers and retailers across 100+ countries, processing $300M+ in annual transactions — and we're just getting started. The gemstone industry is a $350 billion opportunity, and we're building the infrastructure to unlock it.

We move fast, hire exceptional people, and give them real ownership over meaningful work. If you want to join a company at the inflection point of exponential growth - where your decisions show up in the numbers - Nivoda is your place.

 

🎯 Our Mission
The traditional diamond supply chain is broken - opaque, slow, and inaccessible to anyone without deep industry connections. We're changing that. Nivoda's mission is to make the global gemstone market accessible to every business, regardless of size or location. We democratise access to inventory, pricing, and logistics so that a jeweller in Boston has the same power as a trading house in Antwerp.

 

⭐️ The Growth Team
You'll be joining a high-visibility growth pod that owns some of the most critical parts of the Nivoda funnel. The team works at the intersection of data, design, and engineering — and operates with the urgency of a startup inside a scaling business.

This is a new co-located team - right in Boston. There will be some flexibility, but we believe that working together in person will help deliver the most speed and impact.

 

🤔 About the Role
As Senior PM for Growth, you'll own the metrics that determine whether Nivoda grows. You'll lead a cross-functional squad of engineers and designers to ship fast, run experiments, and relentlessly improve conversion and retention across the funnel. This isn't a role for people who want to write specs and hand them over — it's for someone who thrives in the middle of the action, connects data to narrative, and leads their team toward outcomes with clarity and pace.

You'll report directly into the VP of Product and have a high-visibility seat at the table. The work you do will directly shape Nivoda's growth trajectory. You'll have autonomy to ship fast and lead the direction of your pod.

 

😎 What would YOU be doing?

 - Own key growth metrics across the funnel — from acquisition through to conversion and early retention — and drive measurable, sustained improvement.

 - Define and execute a roadmap that balances quick wins with long-term structural improvements to the customer experience.

 - Partner closely with engineering and design to ship experiments and features at pace — you know how to move fast without sacrificing quality.

 - Use data, customer research, and qualitative insight to identify the highest-leverage opportunities in the funnel and build a clear case for prioritisation.

 - Leverage AI tools to accelerate your own work — from writing specs, to running analyses, to generating test variants — and embed AI-powered features into the product where they create real value for users.

 - Tell compelling stories with data — whether you're presenting to the exec team, aligning engineering on priorities, or writing a one-pager that builds momentum.

 - Run structured experiments: define hypotheses, design tests, measure impact, and translate findings into product decisions.

 - Lead your squad toward outcomes, not outputs — you set the destination, remove blockers, and create an environment where the team does their best work.

 - Collaborate with Sales, Customer Success, and Marketing to understand friction points across the funnel and translate customer insight into product improvements.

 - Monitor key metrics weekly, build dashboards, and maintain a shared view of funnel health across the organisation.

 

😉 What do we need from YOU?

 - 5+ years of product management experience, with a demonstrable track record of driving growth or conversion metrics at a B2B or marketplace company.

 - Strong bias for action — you def

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Fine Jewelry (United States)
source_jobs.description:
Location: Fully remote within the US - ideally based in Miami, Los Angeles, New York, or Chicago

Salary: Competitive base + Commission



About Nivoda 🚀

Nivoda is a rapidly scaling global B2B diamond and gemstone marketplace connecting jewelry retailers with competitive, high-quality suppliers worldwide. Processing $300M+ in annual transactions and operating across multiple regions, Nivoda is transforming a $350B industry that has historically been slow to modernise.

Our platform gives businesses of all sizes, from independent jewellers to major retail chains access to the world's best diamond and gemstone inventory, without the traditional barriers of capital, geography, or information asymmetry.




OUR MISSION 🎯

Diamond and gemstone trading has long been plagued by inefficiency, opacity, and exclusivity. Nivoda was built to change that - to democratise access and empower every business, regardless of size or location, to buy and sell with confidence. Independent jewellers are at the heart of this mission. They deserve the same access to quality, pricing, and selection as the biggest players in the industry. That's exactly what we give them.




THE TEAM ⭐️

Our Sales team is the engine that brings Nivoda's mission to life. We're a high-energy, globally distributed group that moves fast, owns outcomes, and genuinely cares about the success of every jewelry business we work with. This is a team that thrives on commercial creativity, deep customer relationships, and relentless execution and we're growing fast.




ABOUT THE ROLE 🤔

This is a rare opportunity to be the first Enterprise Account Executive on the ground in the US, shaping how Nivoda engages its most strategically important accounts in the jewelry retail market. You won't be parachuting into a fully-built function, you'll be helping define what 'enterprise' looks like at Nivoda for this segment.

Your focus will be two-fold: reactivating existing enterprise accounts that have gone quiet or underperformed, and developing new relationships with high-potential enterprise jewelry retailers. These are million-dollar to multi-million dollar accounts - business owners who make buying decisions themselves, not faceless procurement committees. That means relationships matter enormously here.

Alongside new business development, you'll own a portfolio of accounts with real depth, reviewing their activity, presenting new product launches, cross-selling and upselling, and becoming the trusted commercial partner they turn to first. This is a strategic role that requires you to think like an account manager and execute like a hunter.




WHAT WOULD YOU BE DOING? 😎



Account Reactivation & Pipeline Development

 - Identify and prioritise lapsed or underperforming enterprise accounts and build tailored reactivation strategies for each, using a combination of data insight and relationship outreach.

 - Prospect and develop new relationships with small-to-mid-size jewelry retailers across the US, building a self-generated pipeline alongside any inbound opportunities.

 - Represent Nivoda on the ground in the US: at trade shows, jewellery industry events, and face-to-face with key accounts across the country.



Strategic Account Management & Growth

 - Own a portfolio of enterprise SMB accounts end-to-end, from onboarding through to sustained revenue growth - serving as their primary point of contact and trusted commercial partner.

 - Set up regular business reviews with key accounts, reviewing their purchasing activity, sharing platform updates and new product launches, and identifying cross-sell and upsell opportunities.

 - Build multi-contact relationships within each account where possible, deepening Nivoda's footprint and reducing single-point-of-failure risk.



Commercial Insight & Platform Advocacy

 - Use platform data and buying behaviour insights to proactively identify growth levers, flag risks, and have informed commercial conversations with every account.

 - Ac

source_jobs.title: Technical Lead -  Fintech
source_jobs.description:
About Nivoda


Nivoda is leading global B2B jewellery marketplace, Nivoda’s growth trajectory has skyrocketed over the past 12 months, and we continue to expand across new regions at an unprecedented pace. Our DNA centers on understanding our customers' needs and offering them a seamless experience. With a growing team based in major cities worldwide, including London, New York, Hong Kong, and Mumbai, Nivoda is becoming the go-to marketplace for the global jewelry industry.

We aim to be bold, innovative, and relentless in our pursuit of excellence as we transform an industry steeped in tradition.



About the Role

At Nivoda, we are determined to empower Jewelers to sell more effectively. Besides we  offer them flexible payment solutions through Buy now, Pay later credit offerings by our FinTech Team. 

As Tech Lead of the lending division called Nivoda Capital of FinTech Engineering Team, you will play the Tech Leader role in our mission to lead Talented team ,  Architect  world class Credit decision-making platform with our business stakeholders aimed at enhancing customer’s cash flow and Bring Innovative Tech solutions to moderate credit risk.



What You'll Do

 - Design and develop world class  Nivoda Credit Risk and Transaction Monitoring Platforms with help of Engineering Leaders and highly motivated  teams  with Business consciousness 

 - Lead technical decisions, projects, and roadmaps within the Capital Risk Team. You will shape and own Nivoda Capital Tech solutions for managing and serving Credit Risk policy.

 - Provide technical leadership and mentorship to developers, fostering a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

 - Collaborate with product managers, architects, and stakeholders to define technical requirements and translate business needs into technical solutions.

 - Ensure adherence to best practices, coding standards, and quality guidelines throughout the development lifecycle.

 - Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues, providing timely support to internal teams and external customers.

 - Stay up to date on emerging technologies, industry trends, and best practices, proactively identifying opportunities to enhance Nivoda's platform.



Your qualifications and experience:

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

 - 3+ Years Tech Leadership experience predominantly in building / Architecting  resilient and scalable platforms or systems. 

 - Proven experience as a Tech Lead or Senior Tech Lead, with a strong ownership  track record of leading successful projects from inception to delivery.

 - Strong knowledge of any of the Backend languages Node.js / Python, with experience in designing database schemas and queries and streaming data tools such as Kafka.

 - Solid understanding of GraphQL API design principles, microservices architecture, and distributed systems.

 - Proficiency in building Cloud Native Distributed systems in any cloud platforms (AWS, GCP) 

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and a passion for tackling complex technical challenges.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders.

 - Prior Experience in building Underwriting/Risk  or Credit decision platforms would be a bonus.
   
   

What we offer:

 - Remote working in an established team.

 - Exposure to C-Suite at an extremely fast-growing company.

 - Work on cloud-hosted applications utilising major AWS services.

 - Unlimited holiday allowance.

 - Exciting greenfield projects including FinTech (credit, payments, reconciliation).

 - Chance to join and contribute to a company during its exponential expansion phase.

source_jobs.title: Factory Operations Executive
source_jobs.description:
FACTORY OPERATIONS EXECUTIVE

Team: Info Request Team, Operations



What you’ll own

 - Track every order through the full production cycle — casting, filing, setting, polishing, rhodium, QC and share a live status update with the office team daily

 - Real-time visibility on every piece. When the office asks, the answer is immediate and accurate

 - Ensure every order meets its SLA. Track deadlines across all active pieces and flag anything at risk before it's too late

 - Push Nivoda's orders to the front of the queue using floor relationships, get priority without creating conflict

 - Coordinate with supervisors and department heads to unblock production quickly

 - Coordinate with the QC team and have pieces staged and ready so inspections happen without delay

 - Log QC outcomes and surface recurring issues to the operations team

 - Coordinate stone delivery, confirm receipt, match to orders, flag discrepancies

 - Liaise between factory and office on mid-production queries: specification changes, material substitutions, timeline updates

 - Coordinate dispatch, packaging, documentation and logistics sign-off on every piece

 - Build relationships with karigars, supervisors and factory management — Nivoda should be the client they want to prioritise

 - Know how to get things done — when to escalate, when to negotiate, when to push and when to give

 - Be the person the factory calls when something affects a Nivoda order

 - Surface recurring inefficiencies and process gaps to the operations team



What we’re looking for

 - 3–4 years in jewellery manufacturing or factory operations — SEEPZ or similar export manufacturing environment strongly preferred

 - Track record of coordinating between a client and a factory — managing timelines, pushing back on delays, resolving quality disputes

 - Able to navigate factory floor dynamics, prioritise without friction, push when needed

 - Clear communicator both ways, concise with the office team, credible on the floor

 - Organised under volume, multiple orders, multiple stages, nothing slips

 - Proactive, not reactive. Flags issues before they escalate, doesn't wait to be asked

 - Intermediate Excel or Google Sheets for daily tracking and reporting

 - On-site: Monday to Saturday, 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM. Not a hybrid or flexible-hours role

source_jobs.title: Junior CAD Designer (Export Market)
source_jobs.description:
JUNIOR CAD DESIGNER (EXPORT MARKET)

Team: Info Request Team, Operations



What you'll own

 - Create and modify production-ready jewelry CAD models for export clients

 - Handle quick revisions and design changes based on client feedback

 - Maintain export-market standards for finishing, structure and stone setting

 - Ensure proper metal weight, durability, and manufacturing feasibility

 - Deliver work within deadlines with high accuracy and quality

 - Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple projects
   
   

What we're looking for

 - Minimum 3 years in jewellery CAD design for export markets — USA or international clients preferred

 - Fast and accurate on revisions — client changes come quickly and quality cannot drop under pressure

 - Strong understanding of stone setting, diamond layouts, and manufacturing process

 - Able to manage multiple projects simultaneously without missing deadlines

 - Basic Excel for tracking and logging work; clear written and verbal communication with the office team
   
   

Software Skills Required

 - Rhinoceros 3D (Rhino)

 - MatrixGold / Matrix

 - MS Office
   
   

Qualification

 - Jewelry CAD / Jewelry Design certification or relevant experience

 - Portfolio of previous CAD work is mandatory

source_jobs.title: Fulfilment Executive
source_jobs.description:
JUNIOR FULFILMENT EXECUTIVE

Location: Surat
Experience: 1–2 Years
Mode: Onsite




ABOUT NIVODA

Nivoda is a young and energetic global team headquartered in London, with offices in Mumbai, New York, Hong Kong, Amsterdam, Melbourne, Antwerp, and Dubai.

We are a fast-growing B2B marketplace transforming how the global jewelry industry operates. Through our online platform, we connect buyers and sellers of diamonds and jewelry, providing the most transparent, efficient, and cost-effective way to source and trade within the industry.

Nivoda’s growth has been exponential, with over 250% sales growth in the past 12 months and our team expanding from 30 to more than 450 employees globally. We are a team of ambitious thinkers, problem solvers, and go-getters working together to build the future of the jewelry supply chain.

 
 


ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a Junior Fulfilment Executive to join our logistics operations team in Surat. This role will be responsible for coordinating the collection of goods from suppliers, monitoring the platform for ready-to-collect items, and ensuring smooth coordination with suppliers and the internal logistics team.

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced operational environment.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Monitor the platform regularly and compile a list of goods ready for collection.

 - Coordinate with suppliers to confirm readiness for goods pickup.

 - Work closely with the logistics administration team to ensure timely collection of goods.

 - Verify collected goods and accurately update their status in the system.

 - Ensure goods received at the office are verified and properly recorded.

 - Mark collected goods appropriately in the system to maintain accurate tracking.

 - Support the logistics team with quality checks (QC), barcoding, and shipment preparation.

 - Collaborate with team members and contribute to improving operational efficiency.
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 1–2 years of experience in logistics, operations, or coordination roles.

 - Strong attention to detail and ability to follow processes carefully.

 - Basic computer proficiency and comfort working with operational platforms.

 - Good communication skills.

 - Basic English proficiency (reading and writing).

 - Knowledge of Gujarati (written and verbal) is a plus.

 - Self-motivated with a proactive, problem-solving mindset.

 - Flexible and willing to work extended hours when required.
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - A dynamic work environment in a rapidly growing global company.

 - Exposure to an international team and operations.

 - Opportunities to learn, grow, and build operational expertise.

 - A collaborative and supportive company culture.

 - Unlimited paid leave policy.

source_jobs.title: Jewelry Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Jewelry Operations Manager

Reporting Manager: VP Fulfillment

Location: Mumbai, India



About the Role

We’re looking for a Jewelry Operations Manager to drive excellence in production efficiency, speed, quality, and cost optimisation. This role is responsible for ensuring customer inquiries are quoted and fulfilled at record speed, maintaining the highest product quality standards, and optimizing pricing through strategic vendor partnerships and internal efficiencies.

You’ll lead cross-functional coordination between design, manufacturing, and logistics teams to ensure on-time delivery and continuous improvement in service metrics.



About Nivoda

At Nivoda, we’re reimagining how the world buys and sells jewelry. Our global marketplace connects retailers and suppliers across diamonds, gemstones, and finished jewelry - already powering over $300M in annual transactions and scaling fast toward our billion-dollar vision.

We’re bringing technology, data, and automation to a $350B industry that’s only beginning its digital transformation. The result? Explosive growth, massive opportunity - powered by a team that makes it happen.



Key Responsibilities

1. Speed & Efficiency

 - Drive the fastest possible inquiry-to-quote turnaround time (target: under 12 hours).

 - Streamline internal workflows between CAD design, production, and sales teams.

 - Optimise Made-To-Order (MTO) delivery time to an average of less than 3 weeks.

 - Identify process bottlenecks and implement automation or vendor process improvements to accelerate production cycles.

2. Quality Control

 - Maintain jewelry return rates below 1% annually through robust QC practices.

 - Collaborate with factories to ensure adherence to quality specifications and standards.

 - Conduct regular quality reviews and feedback loops to prevent issues before they reach customers.

3. Vendor & Partner Management

 - Communicate and work with manufacturing partners to ensure consistent quality and timely production.

 - Keep track of negotiated SLAs aligned with Nivoda’s delivery, pricing, and quality goals to ensure on-time delivery of our products

4. Pricing & Cost Optimisation

 - Partner with sourcing and procurement to secure best-in-market pricing while meeting margin targets.

 - Support initiatives to increase FARPU by building the right product mix - right SKUs, right price, right quality, right speed.

5. Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with the in-house design team (Corel, CAD) to improve speed and accuracy of jewelry renderings.

 - Work closely with sales and customer success teams to align production timelines with customer expectations.

 - Conduct workshops with key customers to understand their jewelry sales processes and identify improvement opportunities.

6. Data & Reporting

 - Leverage data tools (e.g ThoughtSpot, Excel, BI dashboards) to track inquiry fulfillment, production speed, and returns.

 - Use data insights to drive continuous improvement across operations.



Qualifications

 - 5+ years’ experience in jewelry manufacturing, supply chain management, or production operations (luxury or fashion experience preferred).

 - Strong background in production planning, vendor management, and quality control.

 - Proven success improving speed, quality, and cost in a fast-paced environment.

 - Excellent negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills.

 - Data-oriented mindset with proficiency in Excel, BI tools, or analytics platforms.

 - Strong leadership and problem-solving capabilities.



Why Join Us

At Nivoda, you’ll be part of a team transforming one of the world’s legacy industries with technology and data. In this role, you’ll be at the center of our mission - delivering exceptional jewelry faster, better, and more competitively than ever before.

If you thrive on measurable impact, operational excellence, and cross-functional collaboration, this is your opportunity to make a lasting mark.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (Utah, United States)
source_jobs.description:
Location: Hybrid in SLC, Utah

Salary: Competitive base + Commission




ABOUT NIVODA 🚀

Nivoda is a rapidly scaling global B2B diamond and gemstone marketplace connecting jewelry retailers with competitive, high-quality suppliers worldwide. Processing $300M+ in annual transactions and operating across multiple regions, Nivoda is transforming a $350B industry that has historically been slow to modernise.

Our platform gives businesses of all sizes, from independent jewellers to major retail chains access to the world's best diamond and gemstone inventory, without the traditional barriers of capital, geography, or information asymmetry.




OUR MISSION 🎯

Diamond and gemstone trading has long been plagued by inefficiency, opacity, and exclusivity. Nivoda was built to change that - to democratise access and empower every business, regardless of size or location, to buy and sell with confidence. Independent jewellers are at the heart of this mission. They deserve the same access to quality, pricing, and selection as the biggest players in the industry. That's exactly what we give them.




THE TEAM ⭐️

Our Sales team is the engine that brings Nivoda's mission to life. We're a high-energy, globally distributed group that moves fast, owns outcomes, and genuinely cares about the success of every jewelry business we work with. This is a team that thrives on commercial creativity, deep customer relationships, and relentless execution and we're growing fast.




ABOUT THE ROLE 🤔

As a Sales Development Representative (SDR) at Nivoda, you'll be at the front line of our North American growth - identifying, engaging, and qualifying prospective jewellery businesses, and turning cold outreach into warm, excited conversations for our Account Executive team.

This is a phone-first, high-volume role: you'll be on the phone every day, having genuine conversations with jewelry business owners across North America and building the pipeline that powers our expansion. It's also a launchpad - you'll learn the full sales motion, build real expertise in an industry undergoing massive disruption, and accelerate your career in tech sales.




WHAT WOULD YOU BE DOING? 😎

 - Proactively prospect and qualify new business opportunities via outbound calls, emails, and LinkedIn, targeting independent jewellers, retailers, and diamond buyers across North America.

 - Research target accounts to identify key stakeholders and tailor outreach that resonates, no generic copy-paste sequences.

 - Conduct high-volume outreach with a clear narrative: help prospects understand how Nivoda saves them time, money, and headaches.

 - Qualify inbound leads efficiently, ensuring the right conversations get to the right AEs at the right time.

 - Book and hand off high-quality discovery calls to the Account Executive team, with clear notes and context.

 - Maintain a clean and accurate CRM (we use Salesforce), pipeline hygiene matters here.

 - Collaborate closely with AEs and marketing to refine messaging, test new sequences, and share what's working.

 - Hit and exceed weekly and monthly KPIs around activity, conversations held, and meetings booked.

 - Stay sharp on Nivoda's product, competitive landscape, and the jewellery industry — we invest in your learning.

 - Be a key voice in shaping how we build and scale the SDR function in North America.




WHAT DO WE NEED FROM YOU? 💪

 - B2B sales or business development experience - ideally at a high-velocity SMB SaaS or marketplace company

 - Proven cold calling experience and genuine comfort on the phone, this role requires daily outbound calling and you need to be energised by it, not just willing to do it.

 - You understand what a strong SMB sales motion looks like - short cycles, high volume, value-led conversations.

 - Excellent communicator - you write clearly, speak confidently, and can adapt your pitch on the fly.

 - Resilient and driven - you don't let rejection slow you down. You treat every 'no' as data and i

source_jobs.title: Media Operations Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
MEDIA OPERATIONS COORDINATOR


LOCATION - BHARAT DIAMOND BOURSE - BKC


MODE - ON-SITE


REPORTING - SENIOR OPERATIONS MANAGER



JOB OVERVIEW

We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Operations Coordinator to manage supplier coordination for Media Operations. The role focuses on ensuring the timely availability, tracking, and movement of stones in line with operational targets and company standards. The ideal candidate will work closely with suppliers, team leads, and internal stakeholders to maintain smooth day-to-day media operations while ensuring accuracy, accountability, and process compliance.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Coordinate with suppliers to ensure the availability of a minimum of n+10 stones per agent in accordance with company standards and operational targets.

 - Calculate daily operational capacity requirements and arrange stones from suppliers accordingly.

 - Ensure all stones sent for media are properly listed on the platform:
   
   - For FTP or direct upload suppliers, verify listings are completed before processing.
   
   - For API suppliers, ensure stones are live on the platform prior to media handling.

 - Coordinate with suppliers for newly manufactured stones to ensure certification and platform listing are completed before stones are sent to Media Operations.

 - Verify and maintain accurate records of stones received from suppliers, including total counts and accompanying stone lists.

 - Conduct thorough inward checks for every packet received and promptly address discrepancies such as:
   
   - Missing stones
   
   - Count mismatches
   
   - Incorrect packet contents

 - Share detailed stone lists with Team Leads to support operational planning and execution.

 - Maintain accurate inward and outward records for the Media Operations department.

 - Ensure acknowledgement receipts are properly signed, documented, and shared with suppliers.

 - Collaborate with Team Leads to plan weekly stone requirements and operational workflows.

 - Keep suppliers informed about updates, process changes, and developments related to Media Operations.


REQUIRED SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong organizational and coordination skills

 - 2+ years of work experience in any domain

 - Excellent attention to detail and accuracy

 - Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and follow up effectively

 - Good communication and relationship management skills

 - Proficiency in spreadsheets and operational tracking systems

 - Ability to work in a fast-paced operational environment

 - Problem-solving mindset with strong accountability


PREFERRED EXPERIENCE

 - Experience in operations, supply chain coordination, or media operations

 - Experience working within the diamond, jewelry, or gemstone industry is an advantage

 - Familiarity with inventory tracking, logistics coordination, and supplier management systems is preferred

source_jobs.title: Jewellery Operation Executive
source_jobs.description:
Title: Jewellery Operations Executive

Reporting: Operations Manager, Jewellery

Location: Mumbai, India

Work Mode: Onsite / Hybrid

Experience: 3+ years




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Jewelry Operations Executive who combines a passion for jewellery with a strong process-driven mindset. You will play a central role in managing end-to-end coordination across manufacturers, customers, and internal teams, ensuring every order moves seamlessly from pre-purchase to final delivery.

Your north star: driving precise, timely, and scalable order execution across the entire lifecycle.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 1. Quotation & Pre-Sales Execution
    
    • Calculate and share accurate customer quotations within defined TATs
    
    • Ensure speed and responsiveness, as this directly impacts conversion rates

 2. Order Creation & CAD Management
    
    • Raise CAD requests and purchase orders with manufacturers
    
    • Review and approve CAD files, ensuring alignment with customer requirements before production
    
    • Identify and flag discrepancies or potential issues early to avoid rework
    
    • Organise and maintain all CAD files systematically for easy access and traceability
    
    • Ensure timely receipt of CADs from suppliers and follow up proactively to prevent delays

 3. Order Tracking & Coordination
    
    • Manage end-to-end order timelines, from confirmation to delivery
    
    • Coordinate closely with manufacturers, QC teams, and logistics partners
    
    • Follow up on key production milestones to ensure timelines are met

 4. Exception Handling & Problem Solving
    
    • Manage remakes, repairs, resizing, and order modifications
    
    • Handle customer escalations with a solution-oriented approach
    
    • Resolve issues proactively to minimise delays and impact

 5. Systems & Data Accuracy
    
    • Maintain accurate, real-time updates across all systems and trackers
    
    • Ensure complete visibility of order status at every stage

 6. Process Improvement
    
    • Identify gaps, inefficiencies, and recurring issues
    
    • Suggest and implement process improvements to drive scalability and efficiency
    
    


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

• 3+ years of experience in operations, preferably in a high-volume or fast-paced environment

• Strong Excel / Google Sheets proficiency — ability to build and manage complex trackers, formulas, and reports with ease

• Highly data-driven — comfortable analysing operational data to track performance, identify patterns, and make informed decisions

• Strong numerical and calculative aptitude — able to accurately compute quotations, margins, and order metrics

• Proven track record in operationally heavy roles with hands-on coordination, order management, or supply chain experience. You must excel at managing diverse stakeholders while delivering results within high-pressure, time-sensitive environments

• Experience in jewellery, diamonds, or luxury goods is preferred — but not mandatory; a strong willingness to learn the industry is welcome.

• Strong interest in jewellery and the luxury goods industry

• Ability to think critically and learn quickly in a fast-paced environment

• Comfortable working with technology, tools, and data on a daily basis

• Clear and confident communication skills (written and verbal)

• Highly organised and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities

• Proactive mindset with strong ownership of tasks and outcomes




WHO SHOULD APPLY

We’re looking for individuals who bring both mindset and curiosity:

• Passionate about the jewellery / diamond / luxury goods industry

• Tech-forward and process-oriented

• Thrive in a fast-moving, detail-driven environment

• Eager to learn, take ownership, and grow within operations




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

• Orders move smoothly with minimal delays or errors

• Customers have a clear, reliable, and positive experience

• Operations become more effic

notes: Nivoda is a rapidly scaling global B2B diamond and gemstone marketplace with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
